title: aria chengii
intro: Aria chengii, commonly known as the "Asian Century Plant," is a type of succulent native to China and Southeast Asia. This plant is known for its health benefits, which include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health due to its antioxidant and anti-hypertensive properties. The therapeutic actions of Aria chengii are attributed to its ability to promote relaxation and reduce stress through its calming effects. The bioactive constituents of the plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Aria chengii can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, vomiting, and dizziness due to its high levels of toxic compounds. In severe cases, improper use can lead to respiratory failure, cardiac arrest, and even death. Prolonged exposure to the plant's volatile oils can also cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and increased risk of infections. If ingested, aria chengii can cause severe gastrointestinal problems, including abdominal pain, diarrhea, and bloody stools.
